title: cassuvium subpeltatum
intro: Cassuvium subpeltatum, also known as Cassia subpeltata, is a type of plant species within the Fabaceae family, native to tropical Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it effective against various health issues. The plant's health benefits include treating fever, diarrhea, and respiratory problems, while also being used as a natural remedy for wounds and skin conditions due to its antiseptic properties. Cassia subpeltata is typically prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures.
